candidates.title: ComposeAccel: Binary Composability, Super-Multiplicative Synergy, and Failure Modes of Training-Free MDM Acceleration
candidates.summary: Systematic composability study of 4 families of training-free acceleration methods on LLaDA-8B-Instruct. Primary contributions: (1) first systematic pairwise orthogonality analysis (binary landscape: one synergistic pair, all others interfere), (2) CD-SSD — coarse-draft self-speculative denoising with frozen-token KV amplification mechanism (Ortho=1.385 with EntropyCache), (3) failure-mode atlas with 4 failure modes and proactive detection heuristics, (4) M2 structural incompatibility finding. Full experiments complete; pending targeted validation experiments before NeurIPS 2026 submission.
hypotheses: H1: M2 (Adaptive Step) is structurally incompatible with LLaDA at step-jump > 3x — CONFIRMED (NO_GO, catastrophic accuracy collapse)
H2: M1+CD-SSD highly orthogonal (Ortho >= 0.90) — CONFIRMED, EXCEEDED (Ortho=1.385)
H3: No near-multiplicative four-way combination — CONFIRMED (binary landscape, Ortho <= 0.493 for all non-synergistic pairs)
H4: Optimal recipe is task-dependent — CONFIRMED (M3 best reasoning, CD-SSD best coding, M1+CD-SSD best overall)
H5: KV-cache has critical overhead threshold — CONFIRMED (t < 1.5 -> speedup < 1.0x, t=2.0 optimal)
H6: CD-SSD achieves >= 50% token acceptance at tau=0.85 — CONFIRMED (~52% at tau=0.9)
NH1 (pending): Frozen-token fraction drives M1+CD-SSD synergy
NH2 (pending): tau=0.0 paradox resolution — does partition mechanism add value over naive T=16?
NH3 (pending): SSD+M1 composability — is synergy CD-SSD-specific or general?
candidates.pilot_focus: Completed. Full experiments done: single-method pareto (3 seeds, 4 benchmarks), pairwise composability (2 seeds, 200 GSM8K + 164 HumanEval), failure mode atlas, task dependence analysis, IGSD ablations.
key_risks: Statistical insufficiency: pairwise composability on 2 seeds / 200 samples — full 3-seed replication required (P1)
tau=0.0 paradox: if naive T=16 matches CD-SSD tau=0.0, mechanism claim is weaker (P3)
SSD+M1 synergy: if Ortho(SSD+M1) >= Ortho(CD-SSD+M1), CD-SSD has no differentiated mechanism — but this is a stronger generalization claim (P4)
M1 implementation gap: 1.38x vs. published 15.2-26.4x — must be explained in paper
CD-SSD accuracy retention (35%) is not deployment-ready vs SSD lossless — paper must be framed as analysis, not deployment guide
candidates.expected_speedup: 5.13x combined (M1+CD-SSD, super-multiplicative), 3.40x CD-SSD alone, 1.38x M1 alone
candidates.timeline_estimate: Full experiments complete. Pending: ~16 GPU-hours of validation experiments (P1-P4). Writing: 2-3 weeks.
candidates.gpu_budget: ~16 A100-equivalent hours remaining for P1-P4 validation

candidates.title: Consistency Distillation for Masked Diffusion Language Models
candidates.summary: Adapt consistency model training to discrete MDMs. Train a lightweight consistency head (~50M params) on frozen LLaDA-8B for 1-4 step inference. T3D (arXiv:2602.12262) and FS-DFM (arXiv:2509.20624) partially occupy this space but not at LLaDA-8B instruction-tuned scale on reasoning/coding.
hypotheses: Consistency-style distillation converges for discrete MDMs with lightweight adapter
4-step inference achieves > 95% of 64-step quality on GSM8K and HumanEval
Combination with KV-caching provides additional 2-3x speedup on top of step reduction
candidates.pilot_focus: Train consistency head for 100k steps; check convergence and 4-step vs 64-step quality gap.
key_risks: T3D and FS-DFM may cover this space by submission time
Requires GPU-days for training (vs zero training for front-runner)
Convergence of discrete MDM consistency training is uncertain
candidates.timeline_estimate: 4-5 weeks: adapter training 2 days + evaluation + writing

candidates.title: Batched MDM Inference: Roofline Analysis and Convergence-Stratified Scheduling
candidates.summary: First systematic characterization of MDM throughput under batched workloads. Roofline analysis on A100/H100. Convergence-stratified batching and KV-budget-aware batch scheduler. Addresses the deployment gap contrarian raised.
hypotheses: MDM batched inference is compute-bound (not memory-bound like AR)
Convergence-stratified batching reduces compute waste by >= 30% at batch size >= 8
KV-budget-aware scheduling achieves >= 2x batch throughput improvement over naive batching
candidates.pilot_focus: Roofline measurement: profile LLaDA-8B at batch sizes {1,4,8,16} on current hardware.
key_risks: May not achieve top-venue quality without full scheduler implementation
SSD paper Figure 1 partially covers memory-bound observation at small batch sizes

synthesis_rationale: Front-runner (cand_a) selected and confirmed by full experiments. Key metrics: M1+CD-SSD Ortho=1.385 (super-multiplicative synergy), binary composability landscape, failure-mode atlas with mechanistic explanations. Backup candidates preserved: cand_b has higher ceiling but requires training and faces scooping risk from T3D/FS-DFM; cand_c addresses a real gap but is engineering-heavy.

pivot_triggers.from_front_runner_to_b: P1 full-scale Ortho < 0.7 AND P4 shows CD-SSD provides no differentiated composability advantage AND no failure-mode story strong enough for publication
pivot_triggers.from_front_runner_to_c: Front-runner all results negative and failure-mode atlas insufficient for standalone publication
pivot_triggers.extend_front_runner: P4 shows Ortho(SSD+M1) >= Ortho(CD-SSD+M1) — generalize synergy claim to all self-speculative MDM + KV-caching combinations; this is a stronger claim
